name: Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA)
summary: Singapore's Personal Data Protection Act governs the collection, use, disclosure, and care of personal data by organizations. It balances individuals' rights to protect their data with organizations' needs to collect and use data for legitimate purposes.
keyRequirements: Consent required before collecting, using, or disclosing personal data
Purpose limitation: data used only for purposes the individual was informed of
Access and correction rights for individuals
Data breach notification to PDPC within 3 calendar days of assessment
Mandatory data protection policies and practices
Do Not Call (DNC) Registry compliance for marketing
penalties: Up to SGD 1 million or 10% of annual turnover (for organizations with turnover exceeding SGD 10 million).
